headlineSummary: Anthropic, Google Gemini, and OpenAI drew similarly negative reactions with limited evidence and a close tie, driven by criticism of legal/IP conduct, ad/product strategy, and investment prospects [E2] [E1] [E3].

ranking.summary: Anthropic was viewed negatively on the strength of a comment accusing it of piracy of copyrighted works, with limited evidence and a close tie to the other negative targets [E2].
Google Gemini was criticized for behavior described as the essence of AI ads, reflecting a negative view with limited evidence and a close tie to the other negative targets [E1].
OpenAI received a negative reaction from a mocking comment about investing in it and losing retirement savings, with limited evidence and a close tie to the other negative targets [E3].

evidence.excerpt: > Now, if someone searches for an espresso machine, Gemini will pull up your most relevant products and instantly write a custom explainer highlighting why your product may be the right choice for them. This is like the essence of the evil of AI ads distilled down to one sentence.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly names Gemini and describes its ad-writing behavior as 'the essence of the evil of AI ads,' which is strong criticism of the product direction.

evidence.excerpt: Anthropic paid $1.5B in a class settlement to authors because it was piracy of copyrighted works.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly accuses Anthropic of 'piracy of copyrighted works,' a severe negative judgment on legal/IP conduct.

evidence.excerpt: I can't wait to invest an OpenAI and lose all my retirement fund.

annotations.rationale: The comment explicitly mentions OpenAI and mockingly predicts losing retirement savings by investing, conveying strong negative sentiment about its investment prospects.
